concerns.text: T069a sources ≥100 summaries with 'at least 20 per domain' per FR-031b, but no verification task confirms the final real_world_labels.csv achieves this stratified distribution. FR-031b explicitly requires stratification across five domains; this acceptance criterion is dropped from task verification.

FR-012 requires entries with missing baseline conversion rate to be 'flagged in audit notes' due to reduced statistical rigor. T020 handles extraction but no task verifies that missing-baseline entries are actually flagged in audit notes. This acceptance criterion is dropped.

T096 writes checksums to data/checksums.txt while T076 writes to output/checksums.txt. Constitution Principle IV (Single Source of Truth) requires all statistics/artifacts to trace to exactly one source. Two separate checksum files violate this constitutional principle.

FR-030 requires synthetic validation dataset of 10,000 summaries 'including both binary and continuous outcomes.' T026 verification only checks file creation and record count (≥10,000), not that both outcome types are present. This acceptance criterion is dropped.

Constitution Principle VII requires URL and provenance metadata (URL, repository identifier, fetch timestamp) recorded for every summary. T020c creates data/provenance_log.csv but verification only checks file existence, not that all required metadata fields are captured for every summary.

FR-028 requires Quickstart guide enables audit on 'default GitHub Actions runner.' T083 measures wall-clock time but does not explicitly verify execution on the default GitHub Actions runner. This acceptance criterion is weakened.

FR-032 requires subgroup analysis by 'source domain and publication year.' T050 mentions domain and year but verification only checks JSON file existence, not that year extraction from source summaries is actually implemented.

Constitution 'Verified Accuracy Gate' requires Reference-Validator Agent runs at three specific checkpoints (artifact write, Advancement-Evaluator gate, research_review→research_accepted transition). T100 is placed in Phase N (polish) rather than at these constitution-specified checkpoints. This is constitution drift.

FR-004b requires sample-size mismatch entries to be 'excluded from aggregate prevalence estimate.' T025 verification only checks for data_quality_warning messages, not actual exclusion from prevalence calculations. This acceptance criterion is potentially weakened.

FR-027 requires pipeline must 'either subsample that domain or flag a violation' when any domain exceeds 30%. T044 only mentions subsampling to equalize proportions, not the flag-violation alternative. This acceptance criterion is narrowed.
